div1.entryFree: Remembered, having memory: thy sting is not so sharp as friend r. not, As II, 7, 189. to be r. == 1) to recollect: if you be --ed, Meas. II, 1, 110. Meas. II, 1, 110 and now I am r., scorned at me, As III, 5, 131. if you be r., I did not bid you mar it, Shr. IV, 3, 96. if your majesties is r. of it, H5 IV, 7, 102 (Fluellen's speech). 2) to think of, to consider: O be r., no outrageous thing from vassal actors can be wiped away, Lucr. 607. if I had been r., I could have given my uncle's grace a flout, R3 II, 4, 23. be you r., Marcus, she's gone, Tit. IV, 3, 5.
